query_description: Deduplicate orders using CTE with QUALIFY and ROW_NUMBER()
business_context: Remove duplicate order records, keeping only the most recent order per customer for accurate reporting

expected_columns.name: ORDER_ID
expected_columns.description: Unique order identifier (most recent for this customer)

expected_columns.name: ORDER_DATE
expected_columns.description: Date of the order (most recent for this customer)

validation_rules: Query must use WITH...AS CTE syntax
CTE name should be 'latest_orders' or similar
Must use QUALIFY clause with ROW_NUMBER()
ORDER BY order_date DESC inside window function
One row per unique customer (no duplicates)
Each row represents that customer's most recent order
No NULL values in customer_id, order_id, or order_date
Results can be ordered by customer_id or any column

deduplication_check.meaning: Number of distinct customers equals number of rows (one row per customer)

common_mistakes: Using WHERE instead of QUALIFY (WHERE executes before window functions)
Forgetting CTE WITH keyword or not using CTE in main SELECT
ORDER BY order_date ASC instead of DESC (oldest instead of newest)
Missing PARTITION BY customer_id (ranks all orders, not per customer)
Using RANK() or DENSE_RANK() instead of ROW_NUMBER() (less predictable with ties)
Filtering on ROW_NUMBER() > 1 instead of = 1 (returns wrong rows)
Not including ORDER BY in main SELECT (results may not be sorted)
